data.title: Five AIO bot mistakes that cost you the drop.
data.subtitle: The setup errors that turn a $200 subscription into a $0 ROI within the first week.
data.author: StellarAIO

data.title: What new botters always get wrong.

items.title: Datacenter proxies
items.body: On Walmart, Target and SNKRS, DC proxies eat instant bans. Residential or ISP only.

items.title: Card velocity
items.body: Same card across 50 tasks → 100% decline rate. Spread across profile groups.

items.title: No warming
items.body: Fresh Pokémon Center accounts get queue-priority penalties. Warm for 2 weeks first.

items.title: Single task group
items.body: One module per drop is a recipe to lose. Run 5–10 tasks per profile.

items.title: Missing CAPTCHA budget
items.body: Auto-solvers cost $1–$3 per 1k. Without credit, the bot stalls mid-checkout.

data.title: Why one botter hits, the other doesn't.
Four steps to a working setup.

steps.title: Audit the proxies
steps.body: List sites you cook. Match proxy type per site — DC on Shopify, residential on Walmart.

steps.title: Group your cards
steps.body: Create 5 profile groups, one card each. Set max 10 tasks per profile.

steps.title: Pre-fund solvers
steps.body: Top up your CAPTCHA balance to $50 minimum before a major drop.

steps.title: Warm before drops
steps.body: Browse, add to cart, log in on every account for 14 days pre-launch.

data.caption: is the all-in cost of upgrading from datacenter to residential proxies for one major Pokémon drop. Most missed cops cost more.

data.eyebrow: Setup matters more than the bot
data.title: Get the bot that ships with sane defaults.
data.subtitle: StellarAIO's 24/7 Discord catches setup mistakes before the drop.
